Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reasoning: The course is fundamentally about 'AI & Governance,' focusing on the technological revolution of AI and its impact on 'infrastructure,' directly addressing innovation and resilient infrastructure (Targets 9.1, 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reasoning: A core theme is whether AI's benefits will be 'broadly distributed or accrue to a lucky few,' directly addressing inequality of outcome and fair distribution (Target 10.3).

Life on Land.reasoning: A minimal, inferred link through the general mention of AI managing the 'environment,' but lacking specific evidence on terrestrial ecosystems, forests, or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reasoning: The course is centered on 'AI & Governance,' analyzing 'legal systems,' 'ethical principles,' and regulatory models, directly supporting the development of accountable institutions and the rule of law (Targets 16.3, 16.6).
